aliases: Grape Green Tea with Cheese Foam

english: Grape Green Tea with Cheese Foam

food_subgroup: Cheese tea / fruit tea with cheese foam

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for cheese-foam fruit tea. FOODON_03301099 (tea beverage) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for cheese-foam grape tea. AN0AN (Tea and tea-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 74242007 (Tea substance) is the closest available anchor; no specific code exists for cheese-foam fruit tea beverages.

main_ingredients: cheese foam (cream cheese, milk, whipping cream)

serving.description: 1 cup (approximately 500 ml / 500 g as served with ice)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard takeaway cup of approximately 500 ml, containing grape-infused green tea topped with a salted cheese foam layer, served with ice.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central: Grapes (raw), Green tea (brewed), Cream cheese, Whipping cream, Cane sugar syrup — weighted by typical Heytea-style recipe proportions
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct nutritional analysis exists for this branded beverage. Values are estimated per 100 g of the ready-to-drink beverage by weighting typical ingredient proportions: approximately 40% grape juice/pulp, 35% brewed green tea, 15% cheese foam (cream cheese + whipping cream blend), 10% cane sugar syrup, diluted with ice melt. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries used: Grapes raw (FDC ID 174683), Beverages tea green brewed (FDC ID 171917), Cream cheese (FDC ID 172185), Heavy whipping cream (FDC ID 170859), Sugars granulated (FDC ID 169655). Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Fructose-containing beverages and fruit juice GI values — analogous estimate based on grape juice GI (53) and green tea (GI ~1) weighted composite; primary reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this branded cheese-foam grape tea. GI estimated at 52 (low range) based on: grape juice GI of 53 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283), green tea contribution negligible (GI ~1), cheese foam contribution negligible to GI (fat/protein dominant, minimal carbohydrate). Added cane sugar raises GI slightly but dilution with ice and tea moderates the overall value. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (52 × 51 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 26.5, rounded to 26.0. Carbohydrate per serving estimated at 51 g (10.2 g/100g × 500 g serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~190 mg per 500 ml serving) from cheese foam; green tea polyphenols may confer mild vasodilatory benefit
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension may consume this beverage occasionally, but should request reduced sugar and be aware that the cheese foam contributes approximately 190 mg sodium per serving, which should be counted within the daily sodium budget of under 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar load from cane sugar syrup and grape fructose; estimated glycemic load of 26 per serving is clinically significant
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this beverage due to its high sugar content (approximately 47 g sugar per 500 ml serving) and a glycemic load of approximately 26, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; requesting the no-sugar-added option and a smaller portion size is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cheese foam (cream cheese and whipping cream) contributes approximately 9 g saturated fat per serving; high sugar content also relevant
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this beverage due to its saturated fat content from the cheese foam layer (approximately 9 g per serving) and high added sugar, both of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles and increased cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cloud Crisp Grape Cheese Tea (葡萄芝士云朵茶) is a signature beverage from Heytea, a Chinese specialty tea brand with outlets in Singapore including Marina Bay Sands. The drink consists of fresh selected grapes blended or muddled into a base of brewed green tea, topped with a thick, salted cheese foam layer made from cream cheese, whipping cream, and milk. The beverage is served chilled over ice in a standard 500 ml takeaway cup. The flavour profile combines the bright, slightly tart sweetness of fresh grapes with the grassy, vegetal notes of green tea, finished with a rich, lightly salted and creamy cheese foam that is sipped through the top of the cup. The texture contrast between the cold fruity tea and the dense, mousse-like cheese foam is a defining characteristic of the Heytea style. The product has been available since 2022 and is sweetened with real cane sugar by default, though a no-sugar-added option is available. It contains dairy and is not suitable for lactose-intolerant individuals or vegans. Nutritionally, the beverage is moderate in calories (approximately 340 kcal per 500 ml serving), high in sugar (approximately 47 g per serving), and contributes meaningful saturated fat from the cheese foam. The glycemic load per serving is estimated at approximately 26, making it a high-glycemic-load beverage that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should approach with caution. Green tea polyphenols (catechins, EGCG) may provide antioxidant benefit, but these are substantially diluted in the final beverage. The product is classified as a processed beverage and is not a significant source of dietary fibre, protein, or micronutrients relative to its caloric density.
common_user_queries: What is Cloud Crisp Grape from Heytea?
How many calories in Heytea grape cheese tea?
Is Heytea Cloud Crisp Grape suitable for diabetics?
Does Cloud Crisp Grape contain dairy?
What is the sugar content of Heytea grape tea?
Can I get Heytea grape tea without sugar?
What is 葡萄芝士云朵茶?

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional analysis exists for this branded beverage. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for individual ingredients (grapes, green tea, cream cheese, whipping cream, cane sugar) weighted by typical Heytea-style recipe proportions. GI is estimated by analogy to grape juice from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the ingredient composition is well-documented from the product description and the USDA source data for each component is high quality.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates per 100 g of the ready-to-drink beverage; no branded nutritional panel was available from an approved source. 2. The serving size of 500 g is an approximation for a standard 500 ml takeaway cup including ice dilution. 3. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources at a level that can be reliably apportioned to this composite beverage. 4. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported at a reliable composite level from the available USDA source data. 5. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported at a reliable composite level. 6. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported at a reliable composite level. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported at a reliable composite level. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported at a reliable composite level. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported at a reliable composite level. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported at a reliable composite level. 11. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported at a reliable composite level. 12. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported at a reliable composite level. 13. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in dairy-based cheese foam per USDA data — trace amounts below detection threshold. 14. Halal status is listed as false because Heytea Singapore's halal certification status is not confirmed from an approved source; consumers should verify directly with the outlet. 15. GI is an analogous estimate based on grape juice GI (53) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement exists for this product. 16. The product description notes that sugar-free sweetener may be unavailable and cane sugar is used by default; nutritional values reflect the default cane sugar formulation. 17. glycemic_index set to 52 (low-medium boundary, classified low) as an analogous estimate; gi_category is low per University of Sydney GI Database classification thresholds (GI ≤55 = low).
